title: Week 15
summary:
Capesize
Rates remained at very low levels across the market and any slight gains were just that with owners fighting over five to ten cents. The Atlantic saw more activity this week with finally some transatlantic activity with the Bolivar/Rotterdam for 150,000 to 160,000-tonne 10% cargo reaching $5.00 and some talk that more has been done. From Brazil, rates for cargo from Tubarao to Qingdao had hovered around $10.00 and a touch more.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: Rates remained at very low levels across the market and any slight gains were just that with owners fighting over five to ten cents. The Atlantic saw more activity this week with finally some transatlantic activity with the Bolivar/Rotterdam for 150,000 to 160,000-tonne 10% cargo reaching $5.00 and some talk that more has been done.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: South American grain shipments largely dominated the market this week with rates showing some improvements for trips to the East and transatlantic. Charterers were looking to take ships on a dop basis, with delays evident in Santos and Paranagua, although few delays evident in Argentina.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: A slightly disappointing week in most areas after earlier optimism seemed to run out of steam. In the south Atlantic however, rates did appear to be at least steady as reports emerged of a 2006-built 55,600 dwt vessel being booked by a grain house for a trip from south Brazil to Algeria at about $11,000 daily.
